name: Eddie Cantor
abstract: Edward "Eddie" Cantor (born Isidore Itzkowitz; January 31, 1892 – October 10, 1964) was an American comedian, actor, dancer, singer, songwriter, film producer, screenwriter and author. Cantor was one of the prominent entertainers of his era.

about_quotes.speaker: George Benson
about_quotes.evidence: Eddie Cantor was a Broadway superstar. Sometime he would take too long to get on the bandstand, and they would, “We want Cantor,” chant “want Cantor.” So that became known as the ‘We want Cantor’ chant
about_quotes.doc_title: George Benson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: Johnny was a black faced comedian which was the kind of act presented by Al Jolson and Eddie Cantor.

about_quotes.speaker: George Wein
about_quotes.evidence: Eddie Cantor and Jack Benny and all those people changed their names.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Wein — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Wooding
about_quotes.evidence: Now, the greatest hit in the show in the first half was the three Eddies. That was Tiny, Shaky, and Chick. Well these three fellows had been named the Three Eddies by Eddie Cantor.

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Hendricks
about_quotes.evidence: I had billing. “Little Jonny Hendricks — the Sepia Bobby Breen.” It was a child movie star discovered by, I think it was Eddie Cantor, named Bobby Breen.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Hendricks interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 18, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Whaley
about_quotes.evidence: Eddie Cantor come down there, he sang 'Margie,' Row, Row, Row' and he used to sing a whole lot of numbers and I used to catch them
